compiled_state.title: Brainstorm skill generates Scenario Analysis as informational output but does not incorporate scenarios into the epic spec
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## What happened

The brainstorm skill's scrutiny pipeline generates Scenario Analysis (edge cases, failure modes, user journeys) as part of the gap analysis phase, but this output is treated as informational context for preplanning/implementation-planning rather than being incorporated back into the epic spec itself.

## Expected behavior

After generating scenarios, the agent should evaluate whether any scenarios warrant changes to the proposed epic spec. Some scenarios are best deferred to story-level decomposition, but others reveal spec-level gaps (missing acceptance criteria, unconsidered failure modes, architectural constraints) that should be addressed in the epic spec before preplanning begins.

## Root cause hypothesis

The scrutiny pipeline generates scenarios as a separate phase output but does not include a feedback loop step that re-evaluates the epic spec in light of the scenarios. The scenarios are appended as context but the spec is not revisited.

## Fix needed

After the Scenario Analysis phase in the scrutiny pipeline, add a step that evaluates each scenario against the current spec draft and proposes spec modifications for scenarios that represent spec-level concerns (not just decomposition-level details).

comments.body: Behavioral assertion (Step 5 LLM exemption): After fix, brainstorm SKILL.md Phase 3 Step 1 ticket create template MUST include '## Scenario Analysis' section. Grep for 'Scenario Analysis' in Phase 3 template.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
